because.text: Recovered from an interrupted Kimi Code multi-agent run (session_ccff46ce, 2026-07-18): the audit confirmed, but never wrote up or fixed, that Go jcs() over-escapes U+2028 (LINE SEPARATOR) and U+2029 (PARAGRAPH SEPARATOR). Go encoding/json emits them as the six-byte ASCII escapes even with SetEscapeHTML(false), while Python json.dumps(ensure_ascii=False) -- which is RFC 8785, the anchored canon per GOV-anchors.md sections 2 and 3 -- emits them raw. jcs() feeds the consensus-critical WarrantID / id-soundness / on-wire canonicality checks (soundRecord, keyStateUnderGovernance, parseJSONBlob), so a governance record body carrying either character hashes differently under Go than under the Python oracle: a federation-consensus split between conforming nodes. Latent only because no current vector contains those code points. Reproduced at the byte level (Go a b vs Python raw e2 80 a8). Python is RFC-correct; Go was the bug. Fixed in impl-go/main.go: jcs() routes the encoder output through unescapeLineSeparators, which rewrites the two escapes back to raw UTF-8; escape sequences are consumed atomically so a literal backslash-u2028 is never mis-rewritten, control chars stay escaped per RFC 8785, and a fast path keeps the common case allocation-free. Implementation fix only -- no anchored spec byte changes. Regression: impl-go/jcs_test.go, byte-exact vs the Python canon and confirmed to FAIL without the fix. No differential regressed. Adding a U+2028 governance VECTOR touches the anchored set and is queued for a 2-of-3 governed release alongside the Book III clarifications.

subject.note: Kimi K3 full audit: fixed Go jcs() U+2028/U+2029 over-escaping (was a latent federation-consensus split vs the Python/RFC8785 canon). Impl fix only; no anchored spec bytes changed.
